rationale: Source has feature-shaped verbs ('add', 'implement', 'integrate') and asks to extend existing infrastructure (AbstractClient subclasses + Agent + a possibly-existing PromptBuilder). No bug symptoms. Plan emphasises locating existing prompt-construction helpers, current per-client system-prompt shapes, and similar provider-agnostic flags already in place.

queries.intent: Detect whether a PromptBuilder (or equivalent prompt-construction helper) already exists anywhere in the parrot package.
List files in parrot/template and parrot/generators — likely homes for an existing PromptBuilder.
Read AbstractClient to learn its system-prompt API, completion/stream signatures, and any existing options-pass-through.
Confirm AbstractClient path if Q003 misses; locate the file via grep.
List concrete client implementations to know which providers exist today and which need cache plumbing.
Inspect the Anthropic client — find the messages.create call site to know where cache_control blocks must be injected.
Inspect the OpenAI client — find the responses/chat.completions call site (system prompt sent how?).
Inspect the Google/Gemini client — find generate_content / cached_content call sites.
Locate Agent and Chatbot base classes to learn init signature where prompt_caching flag would land.
Read GithubReviewer — the consumer driving this work — to ground how the cached context would be injected per-review.
Search for existing system-prompt assembly logic (where the agent constructs the final system message it sends to the LLM).
Search for existing prompt-caching attempts to avoid duplicate work or conflicting designs.
Find an existing AGENT_CONTEXT-like loader pattern (per-repo or per-agent context doc) anywhere.
Identify provider-agnostic flags already on Agent/Client (e.g. temperature, max_tokens) — pattern to mirror for prompt_caching.
Discover config-directory conventions (navconfig / Kardex / config-dir) used elsewhere to pick where AGENT_CONTEXT.md lives.
Find existing in-memory file-cache patterns (mtime-based or lru_cache) to mirror for AGENT_CONTEXT.md caching.
Recent commits on parrot/clients — surfaces any in-flight refactor of the client API.
Recent commits on parrot/bots — confirms current shape of Agent/Chatbot and surfaces refactors.
Existing tests for clients — pattern for testing per-provider behavior (helps shape test plan in the proposal).
List parrot/template directory contents — sanity check on a likely PromptBuilder home.

expected_findings: Whether a PromptBuilder/PromptTemplate class already exists and its API surface
Exact file path and class signature of AbstractClient + system-prompt parameter shape
Per-provider call site (Anthropic messages.create / OpenAI chat.completions / Gemini generate_content) where the cache hint must be injected
Agent / Chatbot __init__ signature — where prompt_caching flag would attach
Whether any prior prompt-caching work exists (avoid stepping on it)
Current shape of GithubReviewer's prompt construction (the consumer)
Existing mtime/in-memory cache patterns to mirror
Recent refactors on clients/bots that would affect the design
